title: myrtus auriculata
intro: Myrtus auriculata, also known as golden-leaved myrtle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Myrtus auriculata include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
